name: Shincheonji
description: Shincheonji Church of Jesus, the Temple of the Tabernacle of the Testimony (SCJ) is a controversial new religious movement founded in South Korea in 1984 by Lee Man-hee. SCJ believes that Lee Man-hee is the 'Promised Pastor' and 'Advocate' mentioned in the Bible, who has the ability to interpret the sealed prophecies of the Book of Revelation. They claim that the physical fulfillment of Revelation is currently unfolding and that SCJ members are part of the 144,000 who will reign with Christ. SCJ attracts members from mainstream Christian churches, often through deceptive practices like Bible study groups that mask their true affiliation. Proponents cite Lee Man-hee's interpretations of the Bible and perceived miraculous events as evidence of divine authority. Mainstream Christian theologians and scholars widely reject SCJ's doctrines, considering them heretical and a distortion of Christian teachings. They criticize SCJ's selective use of scripture, its leader's claims of divine status, and its alleged manipulative recruitment tactics. Concerns have also been raised about SCJ's impact on families and communities due to members' intense devotion and time commitment to the church, and allegations of cult-like behavior.
subtitle: Controversial Korean New Religious Movement

claims.claim: Lee Man-hee is the 'Promised Pastor' and Advocate.
claims.evidence: His interpretations of the Book of Revelation and perceived fulfillment of prophecies.
claims.criticism: Mainstream Christianity rejects the claim that any individual is the sole interpreter of Revelation, viewing it as a closed canon.

claims.claim: The physical fulfillment of the Book of Revelation is currently unfolding within SCJ.
claims.evidence: SCJ members point to specific events and interpretations within their organization as evidence.
claims.criticism: Scholars argue that Revelation is symbolic and allegorical, not a literal prediction of future events that can be definitively tied to any specific group.

claims.claim: SCJ members are part of the 144,000 who will reign with Christ.
claims.evidence: Membership in SCJ and adherence to its teachings.
claims.criticism: Mainstream interpretations of Revelation view the 144,000 as a symbolic representation of the faithful, not a literal number of individuals from a specific group.

keyFigures.name: Taskforce on Cults and Heresies (Christian Council of Korea)
keyFigures.role: Critic of Shincheonji, highlighting perceived heretical doctrines

timeline.event: Shincheonji Church of Jesus founded by Lee Man-hee
Continued growth and controversy surrounding Shincheonji

sources.author: Introvigne, Massimo
sources.work: Shincheonji: Examining the Eschatology, Theology and History
sources.title: Introvigne, Massimo

variants: Temple of the Tabernacle of the Testimony
